title: auth/DoA/doa-importer-access.spec.ts:60 › Delegated Organisation - Notification Visibility › Franklyn D (Plant Org Test member) submits → David D (Plant Org Test member) can see it

actors.note: Both actors are members of the SAME organisation (the test's Plant Org Test). The creator step is labelled 'IPAFFS Plant Organisation Test'; the viewer step and the notification consignee both read 'Test Org Ltd' — i.e. 'Test Org Ltd' is the org's trading name and 'IPAFFS Plant Organisation Test' its fixture descriptor. confirmed (trace 065de8c5, actions 3, 83, 87 banner, 90 results).

kindDetail: This is a same-organisation notification-visibility trace. It is the POSITIVE case of the DoA visibility pair: a notification submitted by one member of an organisation is visible to — and view/copy/amend-able by — another member of the SAME organisation. It does not exercise an explicit organisation picker or a Trade Partner badge; the delegated-agent surface it DOES prove is organisation-level ownership and consignee auto-population.
authorizationRules: Notifications are owned at the ORGANISATION level, not at the individual-user level: a CHED-PP submitted by Franklyn D is visible to David D purely because they share the same organisation (confirmed, trace 065de8c5 title + actions 83-91).

A member of the owning organisation who did NOT create the notification can View details, Copy as new, and Amend it, plus Show notification — all four controls render on the dashboard row for the co-member (confirmed, trace 065de8c5 action 90: locators view-details-CHEDPP.GB.2026.1525737, copy-as-new-CHEDPP.GB.2026.1525737, amend-details-CHEDPP.GB.2026.1525737, and row text 'Copy as new View details Amend Show notification').

The co-member (David D) finds the notification by searching the standard 'Your import notifications' dashboard by reference number (CHEDPP.GB.2026.1525737) — no special delegated view; it appears in his own notification list (confirmed, trace 065de8c5 actions 86-90).

The notification's Consignee resolves to the acting organisation's name ('Test Org Ltd') — the same org both users belong to; ownership/visibility keys on this org identity (confirmed, trace 065de8c5 action 90 results: Consignee = 'Test Org Ltd').

PAIR CONTEXT (this trace = positive leg only): the negative leg 'agent's own-org submission → other-org member cannot see it' is asserted elsewhere in auth/DoA/doa-importer-access.spec.ts but is NOT exercised in this trace (inferred from spec grouping 'Delegated Organisation - Notification Visibility').
newSurface: The main CHED-PP run never exercised organisation-scoped visibility. This trace proves three delegated/organisation-level surfaces the standard single-user journey hides: (1) ORG-LEVEL OWNERSHIP — a notification created by Franklyn D surfaces in David D's own dashboard because they share one organisation; ownership is the org, not the author. (2) CO-MEMBER CAPABILITIES — a non-author org member gets the full action set (View details, Copy as new, Amend, Show notification) on the dashboard row, identical to what the author would see; there is no read-only downgrade for co-members. (3) CONSIGNEE AUTO-POPULATION — across Franklyn's entire create flow there is NO manual consignee/importer entry action, yet the submitted notification's Consignee is 'Test Org Ltd' (the acting org); the consignee is auto-populated from the acting organisation. The dashboard banner also exposes a 'Manage trade partners' link (/notification/vnet/protected/notifications/trade-partners/filter) and an 'Address book' link, confirming the trade-partner/address-book infrastructure exists, though this trace renders no Trade Partner badge and no organisation-selection step (Franklyn creates directly as his single org).

creationFlowSummary.submit: Submit notification → reference CHEDPP.GB.2026.1525737 — confirmed actions 77-78

dashboardResultRow.source: confirmed — trace 065de8c5 action 90 (David D search results, before-expect DOM)
dashboardResultRow.rowActions: Copy as new | View details | Amend | Show notification

gaps: No organisation-selection / 'creates for Plant Org N' picker is rendered in this trace — Franklyn creates directly as his single organisation. The org-picker surface remains unexercised by this DoA trace (gap).
No Trade Partner badge is rendered on any page in this trace, though a 'Manage trade partners' link exists in the banner (gap for the badge specifically).
The negative visibility leg (other-org member CANNOT see an own-org submission) is grouped in the same spec file but not exercised in this trace (gap — needs the sibling trace).
Whether a co-member's Amend/Copy actually succeeds (vs merely rendering the controls) is not exercised — the trace asserts only visibility of the three controls, not their downstream behaviour (gap).
